evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Find to my KMP app. Here's my empty Android host screen: FindScreen.kt. Wire up the shared BarcodeFind mode and embed the view.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md and writes a minimal end-to-end MatrixScan Find integration: a DataCaptureContext, a BarcodeFind mode built via the forContext factory, and an Android-embedded BarcodeFindView via toAndroidView().

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is called with a license key
License key placeholder -- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE -- is present
BarcodeFindSettings.barcodeFindSettings() is used to build settings
BarcodeFind.forContext( is used to construct the mode (not a direct BarcodeFind(...) constructor, not forDataCaptureContext)
BarcodeFindViewSettings.barcodeFindViewSettings() is used to build view settings
BarcodeFindView( is constructed with a Context as the first argument (Android construction pattern)
barcodeFindView.toAndroidView() is called to obtain the embeddable View
BarcodeFindListener is implemented and added via addListener(
The gradle dependency com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:core is mentioned
The gradle dependency com.scandit.datacapture.kmp:barcode is mentioned
Version 8.6.0 is used for the dependencies
Camera permission (CAMERA or AndroidManifest) is mentioned

evals.prompt: I have a list of scanned barcode strings the user wants to find. Build the BarcodeFind item list from ['9783161484100', '4006381333931'] and set it on the mode in FindScreenModel.kt. Dedupe the list first.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a Set<BarcodeFindItem> from BarcodeFindItemSearchOptions/BarcodeFindItemContent pairs, deduplicates the source barcode data, and calls barcodeFind.setItemList(...).

assertions.text: BarcodeFindItem( is constructed
BarcodeFindItemSearchOptions( is constructed with the barcode data string
BarcodeFindItemContent( is constructed (or null is passed for content)
distinctBy or equivalent deduplication is applied to the barcode data before building items
barcodeFind.setItemList( is called with the built Set<BarcodeFindItem>
Existing integration code (dataCaptureContext, barcodeFind, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: The BarcodeFindView in FindScreen.kt never actually starts searching and freezes when I navigate away. Wire up onResume/onPause and startSearching/stopSearching to the Compose lifecycle.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a lifecycle observer (e.g. DisposableEffect + LifecycleEventObserver) that calls barcodeFindView.onResume() and startSearching() on resume, and stopSearching() and onPause() on pause/dispose.

assertions.text: barcodeFindView.onResume() is called
barcodeFindView.onPause() is called
barcodeFindView.startSearching() is called
barcodeFindView.stopSearching() is called
The deprecated/iOS-only prepareSearching() method is NOT called from this Android/shared code
Existing integration code (BarcodeFindView construction, toAndroidView embedding) is preserved

evals.prompt: Normalize scanned barcode data before it's matched: trim whitespace, and ignore any barcode starting with 'IGNORE-' entirely. Set this up on the BarcodeFind mode in FindScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeFindTransformer, returning null for barcodes starting with 'IGNORE-' and the trimmed data otherwise, and registers it via barcodeFind.setBarcodeTransformer(...).

assertions.text: BarcodeFindTransformer is implemented
transformBarcodeData( method is implemented
barcodeFind.setBarcodeTransformer( is called with the transformer instance
null is returned for barcode data starting with IGNORE-
trim() or equivalent whitespace normalization is applied to the returned data
Existing integration code (dataCaptureContext, barcodeFind, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: When an item is found, keep the default sound but disable vibration. Configure this feedback on the BarcodeFind mode in FindScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a BarcodeFindFeedback with a Feedback for the found event that keeps Sound.defaultSound() and sets vibration to null, then assigns it to barcodeFind.feedback.

assertions.text: BarcodeFindFeedback.defaultFeedback() or BarcodeFindFeedback() is used as the base
Feedback() is constructed and assigned to the found property
Sound.defaultSound() is assigned to the found feedback's sound
vibration is set to null on the found feedback
barcodeFind.feedback = is assigned with the configured BarcodeFindFeedback
Existing integration code (dataCaptureContext, barcodeFind, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps the built-in finish button on the BarcodeFindView in FindScreen.kt, I want to show a summary of found items instead of just closing.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeFindViewUiListener, assigns it to barcodeFindView.uiListener, and handles onFinishButtonTapped with the found items.

assertions.text: BarcodeFindViewUiListener is implemented
onFinishButtonTapped( method is implemented, receiving the found items set
barcodeFindView.uiListener = is assigned with the listener instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeFindView construction, toAndroidView embedding) is preserved

evals.prompt: Use the Compose Multiplatform BarcodeFindView in ComposeFindScreen.kt to find the barcodes in scannedBarcodeData, and call onFinished when the user taps finish.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the barcode-compose BarcodeFindView composable, passing itemsToFind built from scannedBarcodeData and an onFinishTap callback, without manually wiring onResume/startSearching (the composable owns that lifecycle).

assertions.text: BarcodeFindView( composable from com.kmp.datacapture.barcode.compose is used
itemsToFind parameter is passed with a Set<BarcodeFindItem> built from scannedBarcodeData
onFinishTap parameter is passed and calls onFinished
BarcodeFindSettings.barcodeFindSettings() is used to build settings passed to the composable
No manual barcodeFindView.onResume()/startSearching() calls are added (the composable manages this lifecycle itself)
